name: New-PSDrive

options.name: -Credential
options.description: Specifies a user account that has permission to do this action. The default is the current user.

options.name: -Description
options.description: Specifies a brief text description of the drive. Type any string.

options.name: -Name
options.description: Specifies a name for the new drive. For persistent mapped network drives, use a drive letter. For temporary PowerShell drives, you aren't limited to drive letters, use any valid string.

options.name: -Persist
options.description: Indicates that this cmdlet creates a Windows mapped network drive. The Persist parameter is only available on Windows.

options.name: -PSProvider
options.description: Specifies the PowerShell provider that supports drives of this kind.

options.name: -Root
options.description: Specifies the data store location to which a PowerShell drive is mapped.

options.name: -Scope
options.description: Specifies a scope for the drive. The acceptable values for this parameter are: Global , Local , and Script , or a number relative to the current scope. Scopes number 0 through the number of scopes. The current scope number is 0 and its parent is 1. For more information, see about_Scopes (../Microsoft.PowerShell.Core/About/about_Scopes.md).

name: -Confirm
-cf
options.description: Prompts you for confirmation before running the cmdlet.

name: -WhatIf
-wi
options.description: Shows what would happen if the cmdlet runs. The cmdlet isn't run.

args.name: -Name
args.description: Specifies a name for the new drive. For persistent mapped network drives, use a drive letter. For temporary PowerShell drives, you aren't limited to drive letters, use any valid string.

args.name: -PSProvider
args.description: Specifies the PowerShell provider that supports drives of this kind.

args.name: -Root
args.description: Specifies the data store location to which a PowerShell drive is mapped.

description: Creates temporary and persistent drives that are associated with a location in an item data store.
